summary:
Injury to the superior mesenteric artery results in severe acute mesenteric ischemia with up to 80% mortality due to limited collateral flow. In contrast, the inferior mesenteric artery has effective collateral circulation through the arc of Riolan, reducing the likelihood of extensive tissue necrosis and resulting in lower potential for devastating complications.  

\boxed{C}
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Injury to the superior mesenteric artery results in severe acute mesenteric ischemia with up to 80% mortality due to limited collateral flow. In contrast, the inferior mesenteric artery has effective collateral circulation through the arc of Riolan, reducing the likelihood of extensive tissue necrosis and resulting in lower potential for devastating complications.  

\boxed{C}. Answer choice: B
